---
title: "Exemplars"
---

Profiles of real works, each read as a consistent stat block and cross-linked to
the mechanisms it shows off. Where the species accounts describe a technique in
the abstract, an exemplar shows it doing its job in a shipped game or experience.
Follow a profile's links to reach the articles it illustrates; follow an
article's *Referenced by* register to find the works that demonstrate it.

## Tabletop

- [Apocalypse World](apocalypse-world.qmd): moves and the fiction-mechanic loop, playbooks, and the MC as a designed role.
- [Blades in the Dark](blades-in-the-dark.qmd): position-and-effect resolution, progress clocks, the flashback, and faction clocks as a world-engine.
- [Blood on the Clocktower](blood-on-the-clocktower.qmd): social deduction without player elimination, run by a Storyteller who cannot play.
- [Call of Cthulhu](call-of-cthulhu.qmd): the Sanity mechanic and investigative cluing, with horror encoded in the rules.
- [Ironsworn](ironsworn.qmd): the oracle as a solo resolution engine and progress tracks, in a game built to run with or without a GM.
- [Pandemic](pandemic.qmd): fully cooperative play and asymmetric specialist roles, against an escalating outbreak that pushes back.
- [Paranoia](paranoia.qmd): adversarial-by-design play, a licensed-deceiver GM, and betrayal held inside a consent contract.

## Live games

- [The Crystal Maze](crystal-maze.qmd): zoned timed challenges and theatrical hosting, paced across a long session.
- [Hunt A Killer](hunt-a-killer.qmd): a murder mystery serialised as monthly boxes of physical evidence to assemble.
- [The Mysterious Package Company](mysterious-package-company.qmd): a story delivered as real artefacts in the post, with no visible game layer.
- [Sleep No More & Punchdrunk](sleep-no-more-and-punchdrunk.qmd): free-roaming immersive theatre, the mask contract, and the repeating loop as a multiple-visit engine.
